deep_dive.title: RaLMPH: Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization in Whole-Slide Image Classification
deep_dive.abstract: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a standard paradigm for Whole-Slide Image (WSI) analysis and has achieved strong results in computational pathology. However, most MIL pipelines assume a single "gold" label per slide, which conflicts with clinical practice where substantial inter-pathologist variability is common. Existing multi-annotator learning and label-refinement methods typically estimate global annotator reliability or rely on single-instance assumptions, making them poorly suited to MIL and to localized diagnostic contexts where experts disagree. We propose RaLMPH (Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization), a MIL-based label reconciliation framework for WSIs annotated by multiple pathologists. RaLMPH introduces a reliability field that jointly models (i) local neighborhood structure in WSI feature space and (ii) expert uncertainty (entropy), enabling per-sample identification of trustworthy reference neighborhoods. Leveraging this field, RaLMPH performs sample-wise local annotator ranking to select reliable opinions per slide and applies an adaptive gating mechanism to fuse labels conditioned on local reliability. Experiments on a clinical WSI dataset with labels from six pathologists, as well as controlled simulated benchmarks, show that RaLMPH consistently outperforms existing approaches. Further analyses clarify how our reliability-aware mechanism improves label reconciliation and downstream MIL performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-annotator label harmonization for medical image classification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于医学图像分类的多标注者标签协调
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于医学图像分类的多标注者标签协调
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于医学图像分类的多标注者标签协调
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: RaLMPH introduces a reliability-aware learning framework for reconciling multiple pathologist labels in whole-slide image classification under a multiple instance learning paradigm.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对病理全切片图像分类中多标注者标签不一致的问题，提出RaLMPH方法，在多重实例学习框架下进行可靠性感知的标签协调。该方法显式估计每个标注者在局部区域的可信度，并通过投票机制整合标签，有效提升了分类准确性和稳健性。该方法可推广至眼底疾病分类中专家标注不一致的场景。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对病理全切片图像分类中多标注者标签不一致的问题，提出RaLMPH方法，在多重实例学习框架下进行可靠性感知的标签协调。该方法显式估计每个标注者在局部区域的可信度，并通过投票机制整合标签，有效提升了分类准确性和稳健性。该方法可推广至眼底疾病分类中专家标注不一致的场景。

quick_skim.title: RaLMPH: Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization in Whole-Slide Image Classification
quick_skim.abstract: Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) is a standard paradigm for Whole-Slide Image (WSI) analysis and has achieved strong results in computational pathology. However, most MIL pipelines assume a single "gold" label per slide, which conflicts with clinical practice where substantial inter-pathologist variability is common. Existing multi-annotator learning and label-refinement methods typically estimate global annotator reliability or rely on single-instance assumptions, making them poorly suited to MIL and to localized diagnostic contexts where experts disagree. We propose RaLMPH (Reliability-aware Learning for Multi-Pathologist Harmonization), a MIL-based label reconciliation framework for WSIs annotated by multiple pathologists. RaLMPH introduces a reliability field that jointly models (i) local neighborhood structure in WSI feature space and (ii) expert uncertainty (entropy), enabling per-sample identification of trustworthy reference neighborhoods. Leveraging this field, RaLMPH performs sample-wise local annotator ranking to select reliable opinions per slide and applies an adaptive gating mechanism to fuse labels conditioned on local reliability. Experiments on a clinical WSI dataset with labels from six pathologists, as well as controlled simulated benchmarks, show that RaLMPH consistently outperforms existing approaches. Further analyses clarify how our reliability-aware mechanism improves label reconciliation and downstream MIL performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: WSI classification method with multi-annotator harmonization, applicable to fundus image classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全切片图像分类方法，可应用于眼底图像分类

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RaLMPH proposes a MIL-based label reconciliation framework for WSI classification with multiple pathologists, addressing label noise, which is relevant to fundus disease classification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多病理学家标注不一致的问题，提出RaLMPH框架，基于多实例学习进行标签协调。该方法可应用于眼底疾病分类任务，提升分类模型在标注噪声下的鲁棒性。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 眼底病分类, especially work that connects or combines: classification of fundus diseases; grading diabetic retinopathy severity; classifying age-related macular degeneration stages; detecting glaucoma from fundus images; classification of various eye diseases using fundus imaging; deep learning methods for fundus image classification; fundus disease classification methods; automated classification of retinal diseases from fundus images; diabetic retinopathy and macular degeneration classification using machine learning.

quick_skim.title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
quick_skim.abstract: The LLM-empowered personal health agents with user health (sensor) metrics have offered a promising pathway to alleviate global disparities in healthcare access. However, large-scale clinical deployment remains constrained by an open-ended evaluation bottleneck: physician annotation is reliable but costly and unscalable, while LLM-as-a-judge evaluators are scalable but subjective, inconsistent, and sometimes clinically misaligned. We introduce RubricsTree, a scalable evaluation framework with an expert-aligned hierarchical taxonomy of over 100 atomic, clinically-verifiable Boolean rubrics, evolving from the insights of 4,000 real user queries through an iterative human-in-the-loop curation protocol with an expertise panel led by an experienced physician. A context-aware adaptive router activates only the relevant auto-weighted rubric subset per query, providing the throughput needed for scalable evaluation with expert-aligned quality. Through a systematic meta-evaluation, we show that RubricsTree (i) substantially exceeds a strong large-scale evaluation baseline in expert alignment on challenging open-ended queries; (ii) reliably penalizes contextually degraded responses; and (iii) when used as structured instructions, text feedback, or training rewards for performance optimization, yields up to ~66% relative gains on HealthBench for Gemini, GPT, and Qwen model families. RubricsTree thus provides a scalable, auditable, and evolving evaluation infrastructure required for the continuous optimization of product-level personal healthcare AI.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes RubricsTree for evaluating personal health agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出RubricsTree框架评估个人健康代理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出RubricsTree框架评估个人健康代理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出RubricsTree框架评估个人健康代理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RubricsTree introduces a scalable evaluation framework for LLM-based personal health agents with expert-aligned hierarchical rubrics.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对个人健康代理评估中医生标注昂贵且LLM评估不一致的问题，提出RubricsTree框架，构建包含100多个原子临床可验证布尔量规的层次化分类体系。通过迭代式人机协同整理协议，确保量规与临床专家对齐。实验表明该方法可扩展且一致，为医疗AI代理的评估提供了新标准。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对个人健康代理评估中医生标注昂贵且LLM评估不一致的问题，提出RubricsTree框架，构建包含100多个原子临床可验证布尔量规的层次化分类体系。通过迭代式人机协同整理协议，确保量规与临床专家对齐。实验表明该方法可扩展且一致，为医疗AI代理的评估提供了新标准。
